research.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
research.summary: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. At the same time, government institutions face distinct constraints around security, governance, and operations that set them apart fro
research.content: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. At the same time, government institutions face distinct constraints around security, governance, and operations that set them apart from their business counterparts. For this reason, purpose-built small language models (SLMs) offer a promising path to operationalize AI in…

research.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
research.summary: There’s a fault line running through enterprise AI, and it’s not the one getting the most attention. The public conversation still tracks foundation models and benchmarks—GPT versus Gemini, reasoning scores, and marginal capability gains. But in prac
research.content: There’s a fault line running through enterprise AI, and it’s not the one getting the most attention. The public conversation still tracks foundation models and benchmarks—GPT versus Gemini, reasoning scores, and marginal capability gains. But in practice, the more durable advantage is structural: who owns the operating layer where intelligence is applied, governed, and improved.…

research.title: The noise we make is hurting animals. Can we learn to shut up?
research.summary: When the covid-19 pandemic started, Jennifer Phillips thought about the songs of the sparrows. They were easier to hear, because the world had suddenly become quieter. Car traffic plummeted as people sheltered at home and shifted to remote work. Air
research.content: When the covid-19 pandemic started, Jennifer Phillips thought about the songs of the sparrows. They were easier to hear, because the world had suddenly become quieter. Car traffic plummeted as people sheltered at home and shifted to remote work. Air travel collapsed. Cities—normally filled with the honking, screeching, engine-gunning riot of transportation—became as silent as…

culture.content:
Heaven club neighbour admits offences under Licensing Act, as Met says fictitious AI-generated complaints a growing issue  
A businessman has pleaded guilty to making false statements in order to shut down a nightclub, which police believe were generated using AI.
A Metropolitan police source said the use of AI to generate letters by complainants who do not exist is a growing issue.

culture.title: First trailer released for western starring AI version of Val Kilmer
culture.summary:
Footage of As Deep As the Grave screened in the US, featuring an authorised visual deepfake of the actor who died in 2025
A trailer has been released for the first film to star an authorised generative AI version of a major Hollywood actor.
Val Kilme
culture.content:
Footage of As Deep As the Grave screened in the US, featuring an authorised visual deepfake of the actor who died in 2025
A trailer has been released for the first film to star an authorised generative AI version of a major Hollywood actor.
Val Kilmer was cast in western As Deep As the Grave before his death in April 2025. Production delays meant he never shot any scenes, but the creative team worked with UK-based company Sonantic to create an AI speaking voice based on his old recordings.
 Cont

culture.title: AI is destroying jobs – and the energy crisis could make that much worse | Larry Elliott
culture.summary:
Every wave of new tech has come with a doomsday scenario. But governments just aren’t planning a human response on the scale required 
The transition to a world of artificial intelligence has given a whole new meaning to the concept that capitalism c
culture.content:
Every wave of new tech has come with a doomsday scenario. But governments just aren’t planning a human response on the scale required 
The transition to a world of artificial intelligence has given a whole new meaning to the concept that capitalism can only renew itself through creative destruction. This is the idea that clapped-out technologies have to be replaced by new ways of doing things, even though the process can be brutal.
That has been the way of things for every new wave of inventions

quote.text: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
quote.author: Alan Kay
